Class ButtonController

java.lang.Object
scorefour.controller.ButtonController
All Implemented Interfaces:
Controllable
public class ButtonController extends Object implements Controllable
ButtonController manages a buttons interactions, updates, drawing, and user input handling.

It coordinates with Rectangle bounds, ButtonView, and GameState to render and control a button by changing the GameState.

  • Field Details

    • mouseOver

      protected boolean mouseOver

    • mousePressed

      protected boolean mousePressed

  • Constructor Details

    • ButtonController

      public ButtonController(Rectangle bounds, ButtonView view, GameState state)
      Constructs a ButtonController object with the given ButtonView used to display the button.

      The button also depends on Rectangle bounds to set its size and a GameState for the button to switch to when interacted with.

      The ButtonController also initializes its own AudioController to create sound with.

      Parameters:
      bounds - the Rectangle object size of the button
      view - the ButtonView to render the object
      state - the GameState for the object to switch to when interacted with

  • Method Details

    • update

      public void update()
      When a button is interacted with the ButtonView will update its image to the "hover" button image to indicate that the button is being hovered over by the user. This will be accompanied by a sound played by the AudioController.

      The button will reset to its default state once the button is no longer being hovered over.

      Specified by:
      update in interface Controllable

    • setMouseOver

      public void setMouseOver(boolean mouseOver)
      Informs the ButtonController that the mouse is over its bounds.
      Parameters:
      mouseOver - true if the mouse is over the button; false otherwise

    • isMousePressed

      public boolean isMousePressed()
      A method which provides the calling method with the current status of the mouse press.
      Returns:
      (@code true} if the mouse has pressed the button; false otherwise

    • setMousePressed

      public void setMousePressed(boolean mousePressed)
      Sets the buttons status as being pressed.
      Parameters:
      mousePressed - true for pressed, false otherwise

    • applyGameState

      public void applyGameState()
      Applied the GameState passed to the button's constructor.

    • isIn

      public boolean isIn(MouseEvent e)
      Detects if the MouseInputs is in the buttons bounds.
      Parameters:
      e - the MouseEvent containing the current mouse position
      Returns:
      true if the cursor is inside the button; false otherwise

    • getState

      public GameState getState()
      Returns the GameState passed to the button's constructor.
      Returns:
      GameState of button

    • resetButton

      public void resetButton()
      Resets the button to the default non interacted state.

    • draw

      public void draw(Graphics g)
      Draws the ButtonView
      Parameters:
      g - the Graphics context used for rendering

    • setY

      public void setY(int y)
      Sets the y-axis coordinate of the button
      Parameters:
      y - integer of button y-axis coordinate

    • getY

      public int getY()
      Returns:
      integer of button y-axis coordinate
